Even the most persistent of them in their hostility before that moment was now addressing him with the gentlest words he could find — saying: "Go away, O Abu al-Qasim — go away rightly guided. By Allah, you were never an ignorant man." The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) departed. The next day they gathered again in al-Hijr and I was with them. Some said to others: "You mentioned what [harm] had befallen you and what had come to you from him — and then when he confronted you with what you disliked, you left him." While they were in that state, the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) appeared. They all leapt at him as one man and surrounded him, saying: "Are you the one who says such-and-such?" — for what had reached them of his censure of their gods and religion. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: "Yes — I am the one who says that." I saw a man from among them seize him by the collar of his garment. Then Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (رضي الله تعالى عنه) stood between them and [the Prophet (ﷺ)], weeping, reciting: {Do you kill a man for saying: My Lord is Allah?} [40:28]. Then they left him. That was the most severe thing I ever witnessed Quraysh do to him.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ وَحَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَمْرُو بْنُ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏وَفْدَ هَوَازِنَ ‏ ‏أَتَوْا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ‏ ‏بِالْجِعِرَّانَةِ ‏ ‏وَقَدْ أَسْلَمُوا فَقَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّا أَصْلٌ وَعَشِيرَةٌ وَقَدْ أَصَابَنَا مِنْ الْبَلَاءِ مَا لَا ‏ ‏يَخْفَى عَلَيْكَ ‏ ‏فَامْنُنْ ‏ ‏عَلَيْنَا ‏ ‏مَنَّ ‏ ‏اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَبْنَاؤُكُمْ وَنِسَاؤُكُمْ أَحَبُّ إِلَيْكُمْ أَمْ أَمْوَالُكُمْ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ خَيَّرْتَنَا بَيْنَ أَحْسَابِنَا وَبَيْنَ أَمْوَالِنَا بَلْ تُرَدُّ عَلَيْنَا نِسَاؤُنَا وَأَبْنَاؤُنَا فَهُوَ أَحَبُّ إِلَيْنَا فَقَالَ لَهُمْ أَمَّا مَا كَانَ لِي ‏ ‏وَلِبَنِي عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ ‏ ‏فَهُوَ لَكُمْ فَإِذَا صَلَّيْتُ لِلنَّاسِ الظُّهْرَ فَقُومُوا فَقُولُوا إِنَّا نَسْتَشْفِعُ بِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِلَى الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَبِالْمُسْلِمِينَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي أَبْنَائِنَا وَنِسَائِنَا ‏ ‏فَسَأُعْطِيكُمْ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ وَأَسْأَلُ لَكُمْ فَلَمَّا صَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِالنَّاسِ الظُّهْرَ قَامُوا فَتَكَلَّمُوا بِالَّذِي أَمَرَهُمْ بِهِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَمَّا مَا كَانَ لِي ‏ ‏ولِبَنِي عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ ‏ ‏فَهُوَ لَكُمْ قَالَ ‏ ‏الْمُهَاجِرُونَ ‏ ‏وَمَا كَانَ لَنَا فَهُوَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَالَتْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارُ ‏ ‏وَمَا كَانَ لَنَا فَهُوَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الْأَقْرَعُ بْنُ حَابِسٍ ‏ ‏أَمَّا أَنَا ‏ ‏وَبَنُو تَمِيمٍ ‏ ‏فَلَا وَقَالَ ‏ ‏عُيَيْنَةُ بْنُ حِصْنِ بْنِ حُذَيْفَةَ بْنِ بَدْرٍ ‏ ‏أَمَّا أَنَا ‏ ‏وَبَنُو فَزَارَةَ ‏ ‏فَلَا قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبَّاسُ بْنُ مِرْدَاسٍ ‏ ‏أَمَّا أَنَا ‏ ‏وَبَنُو سُلَيْمٍ ‏ ‏فَلَا قَالَتْ ‏ ‏بَنُو سُلَيْمٍ ‏ ‏لَا مَا كَانَ لَنَا فَهُوَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏عَبَّاسٌ ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏بَنِي سُلَيْمٍ ‏ ‏وَهَّنْتُمُونِي ‏ ‏فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَمَّا ‏ ‏مَنْ تَمَسَّكَ مِنْكُمْ بِحَقِّهِ مِنْ هَذَا ‏ ‏السَّبْيِ ‏ ‏فَلَهُ بِكُلِّ إِنْسَانٍ سِتُّ فَرَائِضَ مِنْ أَوَّلِ شَيْءٍ نُصِيبُهُ فَرَدُّوا عَلَى النَّاسِ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ وَنِسَاءَهُمْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Ya'qub, from his father, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr — that the delegation of Hawazin came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) at al-Ji'rrana after they had accepted Islam. They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we are a people of lineage and kinship, and a calamity has befallen us that is not hidden from you — so be gracious to us as Allah has been gracious to you.' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Are your children and women more beloved to you, or your wealth?' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, you have given us a choice between our honor and our wealth. Rather — return our women and children to us, for that is more beloved to us.' He said to them: 'As for what belongs to me and Banu 'Abd al-Muttalib — it is yours. When I lead the people in Dhuhr prayer, stand up and say: "We seek intercession through the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) with the Muslims, and through the Muslims with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), for [the return of] our children and women." I will then give you and ask on your behalf.' When the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) led the people in Dhuhr, they stood and said what he had instructed them. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'As for what belongs to me and Banu 'Abd al-Muttalib — it is yours.' The Muhajirun said: 'What belongs to us is [also given] for the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ).' The Ansar said: 'What belongs to us is [also given] for the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ).' Al-Aqra' ibn Habis said: 'As for me and Banu Tamim — no.' 'Uyayna ibn Hisn ibn Hudhayfa ibn Badr said: 'As for me and Banu Fazara — no.' 'Abbas ibn Mirdas said: 'As for me and Banu Sulaym — no.' Banu Sulaym said: 'No — what belongs to us is [given] for the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ).' 'Abbas said: 'O Banu Sulaym, you have undermined my position!' The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'As for whoever among you holds onto his rightful share of these captives — for each person [he releases], he will receive six young camels from the first booty we obtain.' And the people returned the children and women [of Hawazin] to them.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمَّارِ بْنِ يَاسِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مِقْسَمٍ أَبِي الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ نَوْفَلٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏خَرَجْتُ أَنَا ‏ ‏وَتَلِيدُ بْنُ كِلَابٍ اللَّيْثِيُّ ‏ ‏حَتَّى أَتَيْنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يَطُوفُ ‏ ‏بِالْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏مُعَلِّقًا نَعْلَيْهِ بِيَدِهِ فَقُلْنَا لَهُ هَلْ حَضَرْتَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حِينَ يُكَلِّمُهُ ‏ ‏التَّمِيمِيُّ ‏ ‏يَوْمَ ‏ ‏حُنَيْنٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ نَعَمْ أَقْبَلَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي تَمِيمٍ ‏ ‏يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏ذُو الْخُوَيْصِرَةِ ‏ ‏فَوَقَفَ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يُعْطِي النَّاسَ قَالَ يَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ ‏ ‏قَدْ رَأَيْتَ مَا صَنَعْتَ فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَجَلْ فَكَيْفَ رَأَيْتَ قَالَ لَمْ أَرَكَ عَدَلْتَ قَالَ فَغَضِبَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏وَيْحَكَ ‏ ‏إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ الْعَدْلُ عِنْدِي فَعِنْدَ مَنْ يَكُونُ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَلَا نَقْتُلُهُ قَالَ لَا ‏ ‏دَعُوهُ فَإِنَّهُ سَيَكُونُ لَهُ ‏ ‏شِيعَةٌ ‏ ‏يَتَعَمَّقُونَ فِي الدِّينِ حَتَّى يَخْرُجُوا مِنْهُ كَمَا يَخْرُجُ السَّهْمُ مِنْ ‏ ‏الرَّمِيَّةِ ‏ ‏يُنْظَرُ فِي ‏ ‏النَّصْلِ ‏ ‏فَلَا يُوجَدُ شَيْءٌ ثُمَّ فِي ‏ ‏الْقِدْحِ ‏ ‏فَلَا يُوجَدُ شَيْءٌ ثُمَّ فِي ‏ ‏الْفُوقِ ‏ ‏فَلَا يُوجَدُ شَيْءٌ سَبَقَ ‏ ‏الْفَرْثَ ‏ ‏وَالدَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو عَبْد الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ ‏ ‏هَذَا اسْمُهُ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏ ‏ثِقَةٌ ‏ ‏وَأَخُوهُ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ ‏ ‏لَمْ يَرْوِ عَنْهُ إِلَّا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏وَلَا نَعْلَمُ خَبَرَهُ ‏ ‏وَمِقْسَمٌ ‏ ‏لَيْسَ بِهِ بَأْسٌ ‏ ‏وَلِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ طُرُقٌ فِي هَذَا الْمَعْنَى وَطُرُقٌ أُخَرُ فِي هَذَا الْمَعْنَى صِحَاحٌ وَاللَّهُ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى أَعْلَمُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Ya'qub, from his father, from Ibn Ishaq, from Abu 'Ubayda ibn Muhammad ibn 'Ammar ibn Yasir, from Miqsam Abu al-Qasim the freed slave of 'Abdullah ibn al-Harith ibn Nawfal — who said: 'I and Talid ibn Kilab al-Laythi went out until we came to 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As while he was circumambulating the House with his sandals hanging from his hand. We said to him: "Were you present with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) when the man from [Banu] Tamim spoke to him on the Day of Hunayn?" He said: "Yes. A man from Banu Tamim called Dhu al-Khuwaysira came and stood before the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) while he was distributing [wealth to people]. He said: 'O Muhammad, I have seen what you have done today.' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Indeed — and how do you see it?' He said: 'I do not see that you have been just.' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) became angry, then said: 'Woe to you! If justice is not with me, then with whom?' 'Umar ibn al-Khattab said: 'O Messenger of Allah, shall we not kill him?' He said: 'No — leave him. He will have followers who will go so deep into religion that they will exit it just as an arrow exits its target. When the arrowhead is examined — nothing [of the target] is found. When the shaft is examined — nothing is found. When the nock is examined — nothing is found. It has passed through the filth and blood [of the target] without anything clinging to it.'" [Abu 'Abd al-Rahman said: Abu 'Ubayda — whose name is Muhammad — is trustworthy. And his brother Salama ibn Muhammad ibn 'Ammar is only reported from by 'Ali ibn Zayd, and his reliability is unknown to us. Miqsam is acceptable. This hadith has various chains with this meaning, and other chains for this meaning that are authentic — and Allah (سبحانه وتعالى) knows best.]'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُؤَمَّلٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وُهَيْبٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ طَاوُسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ لُحُومِ الْحُمُرِ الْأَهْلِيَّةِ وَعَنْ ‏ ‏الْجَلَّالَةِ ‏ ‏وَعَنْ رُكُوبِهَا وَأَكْلِ لُحُومِهَا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Mu'ammal, from Wuhayb, from Ibn Tawus, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) forbade the meat of domestic donkeys, and [forbade] al-jallala [animals that eat filth] — both riding them and eating their meat.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُؤَمَّلٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏خَالِدِ بْنِ الْحُوَيْرِثِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْآيَاتُ خَرَزَاتٌ مَنْظُومَاتٌ فِي سِلْكٍ ‏ ‏فَإِنْ يُقْطَعْ ‏ ‏السِّلْكُ يَتْبَعْ بَعْضُهَا بَعْضًا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Mu'ammal, from Hammad [ibn Salama], from 'Ali ibn Zayd, from Khalid ibn al-Huwarith — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'The [apocalyptic] Signs are [like] beads strung on a thread. When the thread is cut, they will follow one after another in rapid succession.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى الْأَشْيَبُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَرِيزٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ عُثْمَانَ الرَّحَبِيَّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حِبَّانَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏سَمِعَ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى مِنْبَرِهِ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏ارْحَمُوا تُرْحَمُوا وَاغْفِرُوا يَغْفِرْ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَيْلٌ ‏ ‏لِأَقْمَاعِ ‏ ‏الْقَوْلِ وَيْلٌ لِلْمُصِرِّينَ الَّذِينَ يُصِرُّونَ عَلَى مَا فَعَلُوا وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Hasan ibn Musa al-Ashyab, from Hariz ibn 'Uthman al-Rahabi, from Hibban ibn Zayd — that he heard the Prophet (ﷺ) say from his pulpit: 'Show mercy — you will be shown mercy. Forgive — Allah will forgive you. Woe to those who only hear words [without acting on them]. Woe to those who persist in what they have done, knowing [it is wrong].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ رَاشِدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ يَعْنِي ابْنَ مُوسَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَضَى أَنَّ ‏ ‏كُلَّ ‏ ‏مُسْتَلْحَقٍ ‏ ‏يُسْتَلْحَقُ ‏ ‏بَعْدَ أَبِيهِ الَّذِي يُدْعَى لَهُ ادَّعَاهُ وَرَثَتُهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ فَقَضَى إِنْ كَانَ مِنْ أَمَةٍ يَمْلِكُهَا يَوْمَ أَصَابَهَا فَقَدْ لَحِقَ بِمَنْ اسْتَلْحَقَهُ وَلَيْسَ لَهُ فِيمَا قُسِمَ قَبْلَهُ مِنْ الْمِيرَاثِ شَيْءٌ وَمَا أَدْرَكَ مِنْ مِيرَاثٍ لَمْ يُقْسَمْ فَلَهُ نَصِيبُهُ وَلَا يُلْحَقُ إِذَا كَانَ أَبُوهُ الَّذِي يُدْعَى لَهُ أَنْكَرَهُ وَإِنْ كَانَ مِنْ أَمَةٍ لَا يَمْلِكُهَا أَوْ مِنْ حُرَّةٍ عَاهَرَ بِهَا فَإِنَّهُ لَا يُلْحَقُ وَلَا يَرِثُ وَإِنْ كَانَ أَبُوهُ الَّذِي يُدْعَى لَهُ هُوَ الَّذِي ادَّعَاهُ وَهُوَ وَلَدُ زِنًا لِأَهْلِ أُمِّهِ مَنْ كَانُوا حُرَّةً أَوْ أَمَةً ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Hashim ibn al-Qasim, from Muhammad ibn Rashid, from Sulayman ibn Musa, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) ruled: Every [child] whose paternity is claimed [mustalhaq] after the death of the father in whose name the claim is made — if the deceased's heirs make the claim on his behalf — then: If the child is from a slave-woman the father owned on the day he had relations with her, then [the child] is affiliated to the one who claimed him. He is not entitled to any inheritance already distributed before his claim, but he receives his share of any inheritance not yet distributed. He is not affiliated [to the deceased] if the attributed father denied him during his life. If the child is from a slave-woman the man did not own, or from a free woman he committed fornication with — he is not affiliated and does not inherit, even if the attributed father himself was the claimant. Such a child is a child of fornication — belonging to the family of his mother, whether they be free or slave.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْحَاقُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَى ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ فِي ‏ ‏الْحِجْرِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏إِيَّاكَ ‏ ‏وَالْإِلْحَادَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏حَرَمِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏فَإِنِّي أَشْهَدُ لَسَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏يُحِلُّهَا وَيَحُلُّ بِهِ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏لَوْ وُزِنَتْ ذُنُوبُهُ بِذُنُوبِ ‏ ‏الثَّقَلَيْنِ ‏ ‏لَوَزَنَتْهَا قَالَ فَانْظُرْ أَنْ لَا تَكُونَ هُوَ يَا ‏ ‏ابْنَ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏فَإِنَّكَ قَدْ قَرَأْتَ الْكُتُبَ وَصَحِبْتَ الرَّسُولَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَإِنِّي أُشْهِدُكَ أَنَّ هَذَا وَجْهِي إِلَى ‏ ‏الشَّأْمِ ‏ ‏مُجَاهِدًا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Hashim, from Ishaq ibn Sa'id, from Sa'id ibn 'Amr — that 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr came to Ibn al-Zubayr while he was sitting in al-Hijr and said: 'O Ibn al-Zubayr, beware of [committing] desecration [al-ilhad] in the sanctuary of Allah — for I testify that I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: "A man from Quraysh will violate its sanctity — and disaster will befall him — whose sins, if weighed against the sins of all mankind and jinn, would outweigh them."' Ibn al-Zubayr said: 'Take care that it not be you, O Ibn 'Amr — for you have read the scriptures and accompanied the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ).' He replied: 'I call you as my witness that this face of mine is turned toward Syria as a mujahid [in the way of Allah].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَسَنٌ يَعْنِي الْأَشْيَبَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏دَرَّاجٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ { ‏لَهُمْ الْبُشْرَى فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ‏} ‏قَالَ الرُّؤْيَا الصَّالِحَةُ يُبَشَّرُهَا الْمُؤْمِنُ هِيَ جُزْءٌ مِنْ تِسْعَةٍ وَأَرْبَعِينَ جُزْءًا مِنْ النُّبُوَّةِ فَمَنْ رَأَى ذَلِكَ فَلْيُخْبِرْ بِهَا وَمَنْ رَأَى سِوَى ذَلِكَ فَإِنَّمَا هُوَ مِنْ الشَّيْطَانِ لِيُحْزِنَهُ فَلْيَنْفُثْ ‏ ‏عَنْ يَسَارِهِ ثَلَاثًا وَلْيَسْكُتْ وَلَا يُخْبِرْ بِهَا أَحَدًا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Hasan [al-Ashyab], from Ibn Lahi'a, from Darraj, from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Jubayr, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr — from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) regarding the verse: {For them is good news in the life of this world} [10:64] — he said: 'It is the righteous dream that the believer is given as glad tidings — it is one part out of forty-nine parts of prophethood. Whoever sees such [a dream], let him tell it. But whoever sees other than that — it is from Shaytan, to grieve him — so let him spit to his left three times and remain silent, and not tell anyone about it.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَسَنٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ هُبَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْحُبُلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ رَدَّتْهُ ‏ ‏الطِّيَرَةُ ‏ ‏مِنْ حَاجَةٍ فَقَدْ أَشْرَكَ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مَا كَفَّارَةُ ذَلِكَ قَالَ أَنْ يَقُولَ أَحَدُهُمْ اللَّهُمَّ لَا خَيْرَ إِلَّا خَيْرُكَ وَلَا ‏ ‏طَيْرَ ‏ ‏إِلَّا ‏ ‏طَيْرُكَ ‏ ‏وَلَا إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Hasan [al-Ashyab], from Ibn Lahi'a, from Ibn Hubayra, from Abu 'Abd al-Rahman al-Hubuli — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever is deterred by an evil omen [al-tiyara] from pursuing his need has [committed] shirk.' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, what is the expiation for that?' He said: 'That one of them say: "Allahumma la khayra illa khayruka, wa la tayra illa tayruka, wa la ilaha ghayruka [O Allah, there is no good except Your good, no omen except Your omen, and no god besides You]."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ سَلَّامٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو سَلَمَةَ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏خَبَرِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ لَمَّا كَسَفَتْ الشَّمْسُ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نُودِيَ أَنْ الصَّلَاةُ جَامِعَةٌ ‏ ‏فَرَكَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رَكْعَتَيْنِ فِي سَجْدَةٍ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏جُلِّيَ ‏ ‏عَنْ الشَّمْسِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَتْ ‏ ‏عَائِشَةُ أُمُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ‏ ‏مَا سَجَدْتُ سُجُودًا قَطُّ أَطْوَلَ مِنْهُ وَلَا رَكَعْتُ رُكُوعًا قَطُّ أَطْوَلَ مِنْهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Hisham ibn Sa'id, from Mu'awiya ibn Sallam, from Yahya ibn Abi Kathir, from Abu Salama ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from the account of 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As — that when the sun eclipsed during the time of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), the call was made: 'al-Salah jam'ia [the prayer is to be held in congregation].' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) performed two bowings [ruku'] within a single prostration cycle [saja]. Then the sun was uncovered. 'A'isha, Mother of the Believers, said: 'I never prostrated a prostration longer than it, and I never bowed a bowing longer than it.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ سَلَّامٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو سَلَمَةَ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏خَبَرِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ لَمَّا كَسَفَتْ الشَّمْسُ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نُودِيَ أَنَّ الصَّلَاةَ جَامِعَةٌ ‏ ‏فَرَكَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رَكْعَتَيْنِ فِي سَجْدَةٍ ثُمَّ جُلِّيَ عَنْ الشَّمْسِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَتْ ‏ ‏عَائِشَةُ أُمُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ‏ ‏مَا سَجَدْتُ سُجُودًا قَطُّ أَطْوَلَ مِنْهُ وَلَا رَكَعْتُ رُكُوعًا قَطُّ كَانَ أَطْوَلَ مِنْهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Hisham ibn Sa'id, from Mu'awiya ibn Sallam, from Yahya ibn Abi Kathir, from Abu Salama ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from the account of 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As — that when the sun eclipsed during the time of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), the call was made: 'al-Salah jam'ia.' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) performed two bowings within a single prostration cycle. Then the sun was uncovered. 'A'isha, Mother of the Believers, said: 'I never prostrated a prostration longer than it, and I never bowed a bowing that was longer than it.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏دَرَّاجٍ أَبِي السَّمْحِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِيسَى بْنِ هِلَالٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ أَرْوَاحَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَتَلْتَقِيَانِ عَلَى مَسِيرَةِ يَوْمٍ وَلَيْلَةٍ وَمَا رَأَى وَاحِدٌ مِنْهُمَا صَاحِبَهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Yahya ibn Ishaq, from Ibn Lahi'a, from Darraj Abu al-Samh, from 'Isa ibn Hilal — that the Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The souls of two believers will meet at a distance of one day and one night's travel [from each other], even though neither of them has [physically] seen the other.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حُسَيْنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُطَرِّفٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَازِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏يَأْتِي عَلَى النَّاسِ زَمَانٌ يُغَرْبَلُونَ فِيهِ غَرْبَلَةً يَبْقَى مِنْهُمْ ‏ ‏حُثَالَةٌ ‏ ‏قَدْ ‏ ‏مَرِجَتْ ‏ ‏عُهُودُهُمْ وَأَمَانَاتُهُمْ وَاخْتَلَفُوا فَكَانُوا هَكَذَا وَشَبَّكَ بَيْنَ أَصَابِعِهِ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَمَا الْمَخْرَجُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ تَأْخُذُونَ مَا تَعْرِفُونَ وَتَدَعُونَ مَا تُنْكِرُونَ وَتُقْبِلُونَ عَلَى أَمْرِ خَاصَّتِكُمْ وَتَدَعُونَ أَمْرَ عَامَّتِكُمْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Husayn ibn Muhammad, from Muhammad ibn Mutarrif, from Abu Hazim, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — from the Prophet (ﷺ): 'A time will come upon people when they will be sifted like a sieve — and what remains will be the dregs: their covenants and trusts have become muddled, and they have fallen into disagreement and become like this' — and he interlaced his fingers. They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, what is the way out of that?' He said: 'Take what you recognize [as right] and leave what you disapprove of. Turn your attention to your own private affairs and leave the affairs of the general public.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ أَبِي الْعَبَّاسِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَقِيَّةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ التُّجِيبِيُّ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا قَبِيلٍ الْمِصْرِيَّ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ مَاتَ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ أَوْ لَيْلَةَ الْجُمُعَةِ وُقِيَ فِتْنَةَ الْقَبْرِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Ibrahim ibn Abi al-'Abbas, from Baqiyya, from Mu'awiya ibn Sa'id al-Tujibi, from Abu Qabil al-Misri — who heard 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As say: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever dies on a Friday or on the night of Friday will be protected from the trial of the grave.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ غَيْلَانَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏الْمُفَضَّلُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَيَّاشُ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْحُبُلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يُغْفَرُ لِلشَّهِيدِ كُلُّ ذَنْبٍ إِلَّا الدَّيْنَ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Yahya ibn Ghaylan, from al-Mufaddal, from 'Ayyash ibn 'Abbas, from 'Abdullah ibn Yazid Abu 'Abd al-Rahman al-Hubuli — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Every sin is forgiven for the martyr — except debt.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏الْحَارِثُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ حُجَيْرَةَ الْأَكْبَرِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ إِنَّ ‏ ‏الْمُسْلِمَ ‏ ‏الْمُسَدِّدَ ‏ ‏لَيُدْرِكُ دَرَجَةَ الصَّوَّامِ ‏ ‏الْقَوَّامِ ‏ ‏بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لِكَرَمِ ضَرِيبَتِهِ وَحُسْنِ خُلُقِهِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Ali ibn Ishaq, from 'Abdullah [ibn al-Mubarak], from Ibn Lahi'a, from al-Harith ibn Yazid, from Ibn Hujayra al-Akbar — that he heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'The righteous, steadfast Muslim [al-musaddid] will attain the rank of the one who fasts abundantly and stands long in prayer reciting the verses of Allah (عز وجل) — by virtue of his noble character and his good conduct.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ وَهُوَ الْحَرَّانِيُّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُجَاهِدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏يُخَرِّبُ ‏ ‏الْكَعْبَةَ ‏ ‏ذُو السُّوَيْقَتَيْنِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏الْحَبَشَةِ ‏ ‏وَيَسْلُبُهَا حِلْيَتَهَا وَيُجَرِّدُهَا مِنْ كِسْوَتِهَا وَلَكَأَنِّي أَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِ ‏ ‏أُصَيْلِعَ ‏ ‏أُفَيْدِعَ ‏ ‏يَضْرِبُ عَلَيْهَا ‏ ‏بِمِسْحَاتِهِ ‏ ‏وَمِعْوَلِهِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Ahmad ibn 'Abd al-Malik al-Harrani, from Muhammad ibn Salama, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid — that he heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'The Ka'ba will be destroyed by Dhu al-Suwayqatayn [the thin-legged man] from Abyssinia, who will strip it of its ornaments and strip it of its covering. It is as though I can see him now — slightly bald, with bent feet [afyda'] — striking it with his shovel [mis-hah] and pickaxe [mi'wal].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُوسَى بْنُ دَاوُدَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏قَيْصَرَ التُّجِيبِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا عِنْدَ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَجَاءَ شَابٌّ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أُقَبِّلُ وَأَنَا صَائِمٌ فَقَالَ لَا فَجَاءَ شَيْخٌ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أُقَبِّلُ وَأَنَا صَائِمٌ قَالَ نَعَمْ فَنَظَرَ بَعْضُنَا إِلَى بَعْضٍ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَدْ عَلِمْتُ نَظَرَ بَعْضِكُمْ إِلَى بَعْضٍ ‏ ‏إِنَّ الشَّيْخَ يَمْلِكُ نَفْسَهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Musa ibn Dawud, from Ibn Lahi'a, from Yazid ibn Abi Habib, from Qaysar al-Tujibi — that while they were with the Prophet (ﷺ), a young man came and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, may I kiss [my wife] while I am fasting?' He said: 'No.' Then an old man came and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, may I kiss [my wife] while I am fasting?' He said: 'Yes.' We looked at one another [in surprise]. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'I know that you are looking at one another — the old man has control of himself.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وُهَيْبٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي قِلَابَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ قُتِلَ دُونَ مَالِهِ مَظْلُومًا فَهُوَ شَهِيدٌ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from Wuhayb, from Ayyub, from Abu Qilaba — from the Prophet (ﷺ): 'Whoever is killed defending his wealth, while being wronged, is a martyr.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ بْنُ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَجَّاجِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ بَنَى لِلَّهِ مَسْجِدًا بُنِيَ لَهُ بَيْتٌ أَوْسَعُ مِنْهُ فِي الْجَنَّةِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from 'Abd al-Wahid ibn Ziyad, from al-Hajjaj, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — from the Prophet (ﷺ): 'Whoever builds a mosque for the sake of Allah — Allah will build for him a house in Paradise more spacious than it.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏لَيْثُ بْنُ أَبِي سُلَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ مَنَعَ فَضْلَ مَائِهِ أَوْ فَضْلَ كَلَئِهِ مَنْعَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فَضْلَهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from Hammad ibn Salama, from Layth ibn Abi Sulaym, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever withholds his surplus water or surplus fodder — Allah (عز وجل) will withhold His bounty from him [on the Day of Resurrection].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ ‏ ‏وَحَبِيبٍ الْمُعَلِّمِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَيْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُجَاهِدٍ ‏ ‏أَحْسِبُهُ عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمَرْأَةِ أَمْرٌ فِي مَالِهَا إِذَا مَلَكَ زَوْجُهَا عِصْمَتَهَا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from Hammad ibn Salama, from Dawud ibn Abi Hind and Habib al-Mu'allim, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — from the Prophet (ﷺ) — and via Qays, from Mujahid [also from the Prophet (ﷺ) as he believed]: 'A woman is not permitted to [independently dispose of] her wealth when her husband holds her marriage bond.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا قَالَ اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي ‏ ‏وَلِمُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏وَحْدَنَا فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَقَدْ حَجَبْتَهَا عَنْ نَاسٍ كَثِيرٍ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from Hammad [ibn Salama], from 'Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from his father — that a man said: 'Allahumma ighfir li wa li Muhammadin wahdana [O Allah, forgive me and Muhammad alone].' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'You have indeed withheld it [this mercy] from many people.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَطَاءُ بْنُ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا دَخَلَ الصَّلَاةَ فَقَالَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَسَبَّحَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ قَائِلُهَا فَقَالَ الرَّجُلُ أَنَا قَالَ لَقَدْ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ الْمَلَائِكَةَ تَلَقَّى بِهَا بَعْضُهَا بَعْضًا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from Hammad ibn Salama, from 'Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from his father — that a man entered prayer and said: 'Al-hamdu lillah, wa subhanallah [Praise be to Allah, and Glory be to Allah].' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Who said it?' The man said: 'I did.' He said: 'I saw the angels passing it between themselves.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَطَاءُ بْنُ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏الْيَهُودَ ‏ ‏أَتَتْ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَتْ ‏ ‏السَّامُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْكَ وَقَالُوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ ‏ { ‏لَوْلَا يُعَذِّبُنَا اللَّهُ بِمَا نَقُولُ ‏} ‏فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ { ‏وَإِذَا جَاءُوكَ حَيَّوْكَ بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللَّهُ ‏ ‏فَقَرَأَ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ ‏ ‏فَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ ‏}
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from Hammad [ibn Salama], from 'Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from his father — that some Jews came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said: 'Al-sam 'alayk [death be upon you].' And they said among themselves: {Why does Allah not punish us for what we say?} [58:8]. So Allah (عز وجل) revealed: {When they come to you, they greet you with a greeting that Allah has not used to greet you} — and he recited until: {...and what a wretched destination} [58:8].

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَبِيبِ بْنِ أَبِي ثَابِتٍ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا الْعَبَّاسِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ شَاعِرًا ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَاسْتَأْذَنَهُ فِي الْجِهَادِ فَقَالَ أَحَيٌّ وَالِدَاكَ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَفِيهِمَا فَجَاهِدْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from Shu'ba, from Habib ibn Abi Thabit, who said: 'I heard Abu al-'Abbas — who was a poet — say: I heard 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr say: A man came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and asked his permission to go on jihad. He said: "Are your parents alive?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Then strive [in their service] — that is your jihad."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَازِمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُمَارَةَ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ حَزْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُوشِكُ أَنْ يُغَرْبَلَ النَّاسُ غَرْبَلَةً وَتَبْقَى ‏ ‏حُثَالَةٌ ‏ ‏مِنْ النَّاسِ قَدْ ‏ ‏مَرِجَتْ ‏ ‏عُهُودُهُمْ وَأَمَانَاتُهُمْ وَكَانُوا هَكَذَا وَشَبَّكَ بَيْنَ أَصَابِعِهِ قَالُوا فَكَيْفَ نَصْنَعُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِذَا كَانَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ تَأْخُذُونَ مَا تَعْرِفُونَ وَتَذَرُونَ مَا تُنْكِرُونَ وَتُقْبِلُونَ عَلَى خَاصَّتِكُمْ وَتَدَعُونَ عَامَّتَكُمْ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَاه ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏بِإِسْنَادِهِ ‏ ‏وَمَعْنَاهُ إِلَّا أَنَّهُ قَالَ وَتَبْقَى ‏ ‏حُثَالَةٌ ‏ ‏مِنْ النَّاسِ وَتَدَعُونَ أَمْرَ عَامَّتِكُمْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Sa'id ibn Mansur, from Ya'qub ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from Abu Hazim, from 'Umara ibn 'Amr ibn Hazm — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'A time will soon come when people will be sifted like a sieve, and what will remain will be the dregs of humanity: their covenants and trusts will have become muddled, and they will be like this' — and he interlaced his fingers. They said: 'What should we do, O Messenger of Allah, when that comes?' He said: 'Take what you recognize [as right] and leave what you disapprove of. Turn to your own [private] affairs and leave the affairs of the general public.' [Also narrated via Qutayba ibn Sa'id with the same chain and meaning — except that he said: 'dregs of people will remain' and 'leave the affairs of the general public.']

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمَعَافِرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْحُبُلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ الْبَرْحِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ أَخْرَجَ ‏ ‏صَدَقَةً ‏ ‏فَلَمْ يَجِدْ إِلَّا بَرْبَرِيًّا فَلْيَرُدَّهَا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba ibn Sa'id, from Ibn Lahi'a, from al-Qasim ibn 'Abdullah al-Ma'afiri, from Abu 'Abd al-Rahman al-Hubuli, from al-Qasim ibn al-Barhi — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever brings out [his] sadaqa [charity] and finds no recipient except a Barbarian [or unworthy recipient] — let him bring it back.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حُيَيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْحُبُلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ ‏ ‏بِسَعْدٍ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يَتَوَضَّأُ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَا هَذَا السَّرَفُ يَا ‏ ‏سَعْدُ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَفِي الْوُضُوءِ سَرَفٌ قَالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنْ كُنْتَ عَلَى نَهْرٍ ‏ ‏جَارٍ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba, from Ibn Lahi'a, from Huyayy ibn 'Abdullah, from Abu 'Abd al-Rahman al-Hubuli — that the Prophet (ﷺ) passed by Sa'd while he was performing wudu and said: 'What is this extravagance, O Sa'd?' He said: 'Is there extravagance in wudu?' He said: 'Yes — even if you are on a flowing river.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَامِرِ بْنِ يَحْيَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْحُبُلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تُوضَعُ الْمَوَازِينُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فَيُؤْتَى بِالرَّجُلِ فَيُوضَعُ فِي كِفَّةٍ فَيُوضَعُ مَا أُحْصِيَ عَلَيْهِ فَتَمَايَلَ بِهِ الْمِيزَانُ قَالَ فَيُبْعَثُ بِهِ إِلَى النَّارِ قَالَ فَإِذَا ‏ ‏أُدْبِرَ ‏ ‏بِهِ إِذَا صَائِحٌ يَصِيحُ مِنْ عِنْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ يَقُولُ لَا تَعْجَلُوا ‏ ‏لَا تَعْجَلُوا فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ بَقِيَ لَهُ فَيُؤْتَى بِبِطَاقَةٍ فِيهَا لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فَتُوضَعُ مَعَ الرَّجُلِ فِي كِفَّةٍ حَتَّى يَمِيلَ بِهِ الْمِيزَانُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba, from Ibn Lahi'a, from 'Amir ibn Yahya, from Abu 'Abd al-Rahman al-Hubuli — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'The scales will be set up on the Day of Resurrection. A man will be brought and placed on one pan, and all that has been recorded against him will be placed [on the other]. The scale will tip [against him]. Then he will be sent to the Fire. And as he is being led away, a caller from the side of the Most Merciful calls out: "Do not be hasty, do not be hasty — for there remains something for him." Then a card [biтaqa] will be brought on which is written: "La ilaha illa Allah." It will be placed with the man on his pan — until the scale tips in his favor.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏وَاهِبِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَأَيْتُ فِيمَا يَرَى النَّائِمُ لَكَأَنَّ فِي إِحْدَى إِصْبَعَيَّ سَمْنًا وَفِي الْأُخْرَى عَسَلًا فَأَنَا أَلْعَقُهُمَا فَلَمَّا أَصْبَحْتُ ذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏تَقْرَأُ الْكِتَابَيْنِ التَّوْرَاةَ وَالْفُرْقَانَ فَكَانَ يَقْرَؤُهُمَا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba, from Ibn Lahi'a, from Wahib ibn 'Abdullah — that he said: 'I saw in a dream — as one sees while sleeping — that in one of my two fingers there was clarified butter [samn] and in the other honey, and I was licking them. When morning came, I mentioned it to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and he said: "You will read the two Books: the Torah and the Furqan [Quran]."' And so he used to read both of them.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَكْرُ بْنُ مُضَرَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْهَادِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَامَ ‏ ‏غَزْوَةِ ‏ ‏تَبُوكَ ‏ ‏قَامَ مِنْ اللَّيْلِ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فَاجْتَمَعَ وَرَاءَهُ رِجَالٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ يَحْرُسُونَهُ حَتَّى إِذَا صَلَّى وَانْصَرَفَ إِلَيْهِمْ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ لَقَدْ ‏ ‏أُعْطِيتُ اللَّيْلَةَ خَمْسًا مَا أُعْطِيَهُنَّ أَحَدٌ قَبْلِي أَمَّا أَنَا فَأُرْسِلْتُ إِلَى النَّاسِ كُلِّهِمْ عَامَّةً وَكَانَ مَنْ قَبْلِي إِنَّمَا يُرْسَلُ إِلَى قَوْمِهِ وَنُصِرْتُ عَلَى الْعَدُوِّ بِالرُّعْبِ وَلَوْ كَانَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَهُمْ مَسِيرَةُ شَهْرٍ لَمُلِئَ مِنْهُ رُعْبًا وَأُحِلَّتْ لِي الْغَنَائِمُ آكُلُهَا وَكَانَ مَنْ قَبْلِي يُعَظِّمُونَ أَكْلَهَا كَانُوا يُحْرِقُونَهَا وَجُعِلَتْ لِي الْأَرْضُ مَسَاجِدَ وَطَهُورًا أَيْنَمَا أَدْرَكَتْنِي الصَّلَاةُ تَمَسَّحْتُ وَصَلَّيْتُ وَكَانَ مَنْ قَبْلِي يُعَظِّمُونَ ذَلِكَ إِنَّمَا كَانُوا يُصَلُّونَ فِي كَنَائِسِهِمْ وَبِيَعِهِمْ وَالْخَامِسَةُ هِيَ مَا هِيَ قِيلَ لِي ‏ ‏سَلْ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّ كُلَّ نَبِيٍّ قَدْ سَأَلَ فَأَخَّرْتُ مَسْأَلَتِي إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ فَهِيَ لَكُمْ وَلِمَنْ شَهِدَ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba ibn Sa'id, from Bakr ibn Mudar, from Ibn al-Had, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that in the year of the Expedition of Tabuk, the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) rose at night to pray. A group of his Companions gathered behind him to guard him. When he had prayed and turned toward them, he said to them: 'Tonight I have been given five things that no one before me has been given. As for me — I have been sent to all of mankind universally, whereas those before me were sent only to their own people. I have been aided against the enemy with terror [al-ru'b], even if there were a month's journey between me and them — it would be filled with terror [of me]. The spoils of war have been made lawful for me to use, whereas those before me considered it a grave matter — they would burn them. The earth has been made for me a place of prostration and a means of purification — wherever prayer overtakes me, I perform tayammum and pray; whereas those before me considered this a great thing — they would only pray in their churches and synagogues. And the fifth — it is what it is. I was told: "Ask — for every prophet has asked." So I have deferred my request until the Day of Resurrection, and it is for you and for all who bear witness that there is no god but Allah.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏رِشْدِينُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَجَّاجِ بْنِ شَدَّادٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي صَالِحٍ الْغِفَارِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَوَّلُ مَنْ يَدْخُلُ مِنْ هَذَا الْبَابِ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ فَدَخَلَ ‏ ‏سَعْدُ بْنُ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba ibn Sa'id, from Rishdayn, from al-Hajjaj ibn Shaddad, from Abu Salih al-Ghifari — that the Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The first to enter through this door will be a man from the people of Paradise.' And Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas entered.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏رِشْدِينُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنِ بْنِ ثَوْبَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِشَامِ بْنِ أَبِي رُقَيَّةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا ‏ ‏عَدْوَى ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏طِيَرَةَ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏هَامَةَ ‏ ‏وَلَا حَسَدَ ‏ ‏وَالْعَيْنُ ‏ ‏حَقٌّ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba, from Rishdayn ibn Sa'd, from al-Hasan ibn Thawban, from Hisham ibn Abi Ruqayya — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'There is no [unlawful] contagion ['adwa], no evil omen [tiyara], no hama [the owl harboring the spirit of the slain, a pre-Islamic superstition], and no [unlawful] envy — but the evil eye is real.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَأَلْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ هَلْ تُحِسُّ بِالْوَحْيِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نَعَمْ أَسْمَعُ ‏ ‏صَلَاصِلَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ أَسْكُتُ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ فَمَا مِنْ مَرَّةٍ يُوحَى إِلَيَّ إِلَّا ظَنَنْتُ أَنَّ نَفْسِي تَفِيضُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba, from Ibn Lahi'a, from Yazid ibn Abi Habib, from 'Amr ibn al-Walid — that 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr said: 'I asked the Prophet (ﷺ): "O Messenger of Allah, do you sense the revelation when it comes?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: "Yes — I hear a clanging [sound, like bells — salasil], and then I fall silent at that. And every time revelation comes to me, I believe my soul is about to depart [from my body]."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَارِثِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جُنْدُبِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عَوْفٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَطَلَعَتْ الشَّمْسُ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَأْتِي اللَّهَ قَوْمٌ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ نُورُهُمْ كَنُورِ الشَّمْسِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏أَنَحْنُ هُمْ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ لَا وَلَكُمْ خَيْرٌ كَثِيرٌ وَلَكِنَّهُمْ الْفُقَرَاءُ ‏ ‏وَالْمُهَاجِرُونَ ‏ ‏الَّذِينَ يُحْشَرُونَ مِنْ أَقْطَارِ الْأَرْضِ وَقَالَ ‏ ‏طُوبَى ‏ ‏لِلْغُرَبَاءِ ‏ ‏طُوبَى ‏ ‏لِلْغُرَبَاءِ ‏ ‏طُوبَى ‏ ‏لِلْغُرَبَاءِ فَقِيلَ مَنْ الْغُرَبَاءُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ نَاسٌ صَالِحُونَ فِي نَاسِ سَوْءٍ كَثِيرٍ مَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْصِيهِمْ أَكْثَرُ مِمَّنْ يُطِيعُهُمْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Qutayba, from Ibn Lahi'a, from al-Harith ibn Yazid, from Jundub ibn 'Abdullah, from Sufyan ibn 'Awf — that he said: 'I was with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) when the sun rose. He said: "A people will come before Allah on the Day of Resurrection whose light is like the light of the sun." Abu Bakr said: "Are we they, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "No — but you have abundant good. Rather, they are the poor [fuqara'] and the emigrants [muhajirun] who will be gathered from every corner of the earth." And he said: "Tuba [glad tidings] to the strangers! Tuba to the strangers! Tuba to the strangers!" It was said: "Who are the strangers, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Righteous people among vast numbers of evil people — those who disobey them are more than those who follow them."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏يَبْلُغُ بِهِ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ لَمْ يَرْحَمْ صَغِيرَنَا وَيَعْرِفْ حَقَّ كَبِيرِنَا فَلَيْسَ مِنَّا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Ali ibn 'Abdullah, from Sufyan, from Ibn Abi Najih, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amir — from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr, traced back to the Prophet (ﷺ): 'Whoever does not show mercy to our young and does not recognize the right of our elders is not of us.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وَسَمِعْتُهُ ‏ ‏أَنَا مِنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بَيْنَمَا رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏يَتَبَخْتَرُ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏حُلَّةٍ ‏ ‏إِذْ أَمَرَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ بِهِ الْأَرْضَ ‏ ‏فَأَخَذَتْهُ فَهُوَ ‏ ‏يَتَجَلْجَلُ ‏ ‏فِيهَا أَوْ ‏ ‏يَتَجَرْجَرُ ‏ ‏فِيهَا إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Abdullah ibn Muhammad [ibn Abi Shayba], from Ibn Fudayl, from 'Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from his father — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'While a man was strutting arrogantly in a fine garment, Allah (عز وجل) commanded the earth [to seize him], and it took hold of him — and he will be sinking [being swallowed] into it, or churning about in it, until the Day of Resurrection.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَارُونُ بْنُ مَعْرُوفٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ وَهْبٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أُسَامَةُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَمْرَو بْنَ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَهُ عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا جَاءَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنِّي أَنْزِعُ فِي حَوْضِي حَتَّى إِذَا مَلَأْتُهُ لِأَهْلِي وَرَدَ عَلَيَّ الْبَعِيرُ لِغَيْرِي فَسَقَيْتُهُ فَهَلْ لِي فِي ذَلِكَ مِنْ أَجْرٍ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي كُلِّ ‏ ‏ذَاتِ كَبِدٍ ‏ ‏حَرَّى ‏ ‏أَجْرٌ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Harun ibn Ma'ruf, from 'Abdullah ibn Wahb, from Usama — that 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb narrated to him from his father, from his grandfather — that a man came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and said: 'I draw water into my trough until I fill it for my family, and then a camel belonging to someone else comes to me and I water it. Is there any reward in that for me?' The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'For every living creature with a thirsty liver [i.e., every thirsty being], there is a reward.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْجَبَّارِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ يَعْنِي الْخَطَّابِيَّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏بَقِيَّةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ الزُّبَيْدِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ لِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ مَسَّ ذَكَرَهُ فَلْيَتَوَضَّأْ وَأَيُّمَا امْرَأَةٍ مَسَّتْ فَرْجَهَا فَلْتَتَوَضَّأْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Abd al-Jabbar ibn Muhammad al-Khattabi, from Baqiyya, from Muhammad ibn al-Walid al-Zubaidi, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said to him: 'Whoever touches his private part [dhakr] — let him perform wudu. And any woman who touches her private part [farj] — let her perform wudu.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَمَّامٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قَتَادَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقْتُ صَلَاةِ الظُّهْرِ إِذَا زَالَتْ الشَّمْسُ وَكَانَ ظِلُّ الرَّجُلِ كَطُولِهِ مَا لَمْ تَحْضُرْ الْعَصْرُ وَوَقْتُ صَلَاةِ الْعَصْرِ مَا لَمْ تَصْفَرَّ الشَّمْسُ وَوَقْتُ صَلَاةِ الْمَغْرِبِ مَا لَمْ يَغِبْ ‏ ‏الشَّفَقُ ‏ ‏وَوَقْتُ صَلَاةِ الْعِشَاءِ إِلَى نِصْفِ اللَّيْلِ وَوَقْتُ صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ مِنْ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ مَا لَمْ تَطْلُعْ الشَّمْسُ فَإِذَا طَلَعَتْ فَأَمْسِكْ فَإِنَّهَا تَطْلُعُ بَيْنَ ‏ ‏قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ مَعَ ‏ ‏قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Affan, from Hammam, from Qatada, from Abu Ayyub — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'The time of Dhuhr prayer is when the sun declines [past noon] and a man's shadow equals his height — as long as 'Asr has not yet entered. The time of 'Asr prayer is as long as the sun has not yet turned yellow. The time of Maghrib prayer is as long as the twilight [al-shafaq] has not yet disappeared. The time of 'Isha' prayer is until the middle of the night. And the time of Fajr prayer is from when dawn breaks until the sun rises — and when it rises, refrain from prayer, for it rises between — or alongside — the two horns of Shaytan.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ حَمَّادٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عَوَانَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عُثْمَانُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي حَرْبٍ الدَّيْلِيِّ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا أَظَلَّتْ ‏ ‏الْخَضْرَاءُ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏أَقَلَّتْ ‏ ‏الْغَبْرَاءُ ‏ ‏مِنْ رَجُلٍ أَصْدَقَ لَهْجَةً مِنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي ذَرٍّ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Yahya ibn Hammad, from Abu 'Awana, from al-A'mash, from 'Uthman ibn Qays, from Abu Harb al-Dayli — who heard 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As say: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'The sky has not shaded over, nor the earth carried, a man more truthful in speech than Abu Dharr.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ ‏ ‏وَأَبُو النَّضْرِ ‏ ‏قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏زُهَيْرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُهَاجِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بَابَاهُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَذُكِرَتْ الْأَعْمَالُ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَا مِنْ أَيَّامٍ الْعَمَلُ فِيهِنَّ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْعَشْرِ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَلَا الْجِهَادُ قَالَ فَأَكْبَرَهُ قَالَ وَلَا الْجِهَادُ إِلَّا أَنْ يَخْرُجَ رَجُلٌ بِنَفْسِهِ وَمَالِهِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ تَكُونَ ‏ ‏مُهْجَةُ ‏ ‏نَفْسِهِ فِيهِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Yahya ibn Adam and Abu al-Nadr, from Zuhayr, from Ibrahim ibn Muhajir, from 'Abdullah ibn Babah — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, when deeds were mentioned in his presence: 'There are no days in which deeds are more excellent than these ten days [the first ten days of Dhul-Hijja].' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah — not even jihad?' He considered it [jihad] a great thing [but still said:] 'Not even jihad — except for a man who goes out with himself and his wealth in the way of Allah (عز وجل), and his very life [muhja] is consumed in it.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏السَّائِبِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَمَّا تُوُفِّيَ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ ابْنُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَسَفَتْ الشَّمْسُ فَقَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ فَأَطَالَ الْقِيَامَ ثُمَّ رَكَعَ مِثْلَ قِيَامِهِ ثُمَّ سَجَدَ مِثْلَ رُكُوعِهِ فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ كَذَلِكَ ثُمَّ سَلَّمَ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Yahya ibn Adam, from Abu Bakr, from Abu Ishaq, from al-Sa'ib ibn Malik — that when Ibrahim, the son of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), died, the sun eclipsed. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) stood and prayed two rak'ahs — prolonging the standing, then bowing as long as his standing, then prostrating as long as his bowing. He prayed two rak'ahs like that, then gave salam.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏شُرَحْبِيلُ بْنُ شَرِيكٍ الْمَعَافِرِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ رَافِعٍ التَّنُوخِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَا أُبَالِي مَا أَتَيْتُ ‏ ‏أَوْ مَا رَكِبْتُ ‏ ‏إِذَا أَنَا شَرِبْتُ ‏ ‏تِرْيَاقًا ‏ ‏أَوْ تَعَلَّقْتُ ‏ ‏تَمِيمَةً ‏ ‏أَوْ قُلْتُ الشِّعْرَ مِنْ قِبَلِ نَفْسِي ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Abdullah ibn Yazid, from Sa'id ibn Abi Ayyub, from Shurahbil ibn Sharik al-Ma'afiri, from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Rafi' al-Tanukhi — who heard 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As say: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'I care not what I do — or what I ride upon — when I have drunk tiryaq [an antidote/medicine containing impermissible ingredients], or hung a tamima [protective amulet], or composed poetry from my own whim [i.e., these actions invalidate or negate one's state — a warning against them].'

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَيْوَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏رَبِيعَةُ بْنُ سَيْفٍ الْمَعَافِرِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْحُبُلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ رَأَى ‏ ‏فَاطِمَةَ ‏ ‏ابْنَتَهُ فَقَالَ لَهَا مِنْ أَيْنَ أَقْبَلْتِ قَالَتْ أَقْبَلْتُ مِنْ وَرَاءِ جَنَازَةِ هَذَا الرَّجُلِ قَالَ فَهَلْ بَلَغْتِ مَعَهُمْ ‏ ‏الْكُدَى ‏ ‏قَالَتْ لَا وَكَيْفَ أَبْلُغُهَا وَقَدْ سَمِعْتُ مِنْكَ مَا سَمِعْتُ قَالَ وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ ‏ ‏لَوْ بَلَغْتِ مَعَهُمْ ‏ ‏الْكُدَى ‏ ‏مَا رَأَيْتِ الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى يَرَاهَا جَدُّ أَبِيكِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Abdullah ibn Yazid, from Haywa, from Rabi'a ibn Sayf al-Ma'afiri, from Abu 'Abd al-Rahman al-Hubuli — from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) that he saw his daughter Fatima [coming] and said to her: 'Where have you come from?' She said: 'I came from [following] the funeral of this man.' He said: 'Did you reach al-Kuda [the graveyard] with them?' She said: 'No — and how could I reach it when I have heard from you what I have heard?' He said: 'By the One in whose hand is my soul — had you reached al-Kuda with them, you would not see Paradise until your father's grandfather sees it [first].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَيَّاشِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ الْقِتْبَانِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عِيسَى بْنَ هِلَالٍ الصَّدَفِيَّ ‏ ‏وَأَبَا عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْحُبُلِيَّ ‏ ‏يَقُولَانِ سَمِعْنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏سَيَكُونُ فِي آخِرِ أُمَّتِي رِجَالٌ يَرْكَبُونَ عَلَى ‏ ‏السُّرُوجِ ‏ ‏كَأَشْبَاهِ الرِّجَالِ يَنْزِلُونَ عَلَى أَبْوَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ نِسَاؤُهُمْ كَاسِيَاتٌ عَارِيَاتٌ عَلَى رُءُوسِهِمْ ‏ ‏كَأَسْنِمَةِ ‏ ‏الْبُخْتِ ‏ ‏الْعِجَافِ ‏ ‏الْعَنُوهُنَّ فَإِنَّهُنَّ مَلْعُونَاتٌ لَوْ كَانَتْ وَرَاءَكُمْ أُمَّةٌ مِنْ الْأُمَمِ لَخَدَمْنَ نِسَاؤُكُمْ نِسَاءَهُمْ كَمَا يَخْدِمْنَكُمْ نِسَاءُ الْأُمَمِ قَبْلَكُمْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Abdullah ibn Yazid, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Ayyash ibn 'Abbas al-Qitbani, from his father, who heard 'Isa ibn Hilal al-Sadafi and Abu 'Abd al-Rahman al-Hubuli say: We heard 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr say: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'In the latter times of my ummah there will be men who ride on saddles resembling men [in appearance only] who dismount at the doors of the mosque. Their women are clothed yet bare [kasiyat 'ariyat], with things on their heads like the humps of emaciated Bactrian camels. Curse them — for they are accursed. If there were a nation after you [in succession], your women would serve their women just as the women of the nations before you serve you.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو الْأَسْوَدِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَنْ قُتِلَ دُونَ مَالِهِ مَظْلُومًا فَلَهُ الْجَنَّةُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Abdullah ibn Yazid, from Sa'id ibn Abi Ayyub, from Abu al-Aswad, from 'Ikrima the freed slave of Ibn 'Abbas — that he heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'Whoever is killed defending his wealth, while being wronged — Paradise is his.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ سَمَّعَ النَّاسَ بِعَمَلِهِ ‏ ‏سَمَّعَ اللَّهُ بِهِ سَامِعَ خَلْقِهِ وَحَقَّرَهُ وَصَغَّرَهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Muhammad ibn 'Ubayd, from al-A'mash, from 'Amr ibn Murra, from Abu Yazid — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever causes people to hear of his deeds [for show] — Allah will make the listeners of His creation hear of him [to his disgrace], and will belittle him and humiliate him.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏زَكَرِيَّا ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏الْمُسْلِمُ مَنْ سَلِمَ النَّاسُ مِنْ لِسَانِهِ وَيَدِهِ وَالْمُهَاجِرُ مَنْ هَجَرَ مَا نَهَى اللَّهُ عَنْهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Muhammad ibn 'Ubayd, from Zakariyya, from 'Amir — who heard 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr say: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say: 'The Muslim is one from whose tongue and hand people are safe. And the muhajir [true emigrant] is one who abandons what Allah has forbidden.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَارِمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُعْتَمِرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو الْعَلَاءِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُطَرِّفٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي رَبِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ذَكَرْتُ لِلنَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الصَّوْمَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏صُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ عَشَرَةِ أَيَّامٍ يَوْمًا وَلَكَ أَجْرُ التِّسْعَةِ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ إِنِّي أَقْوَى مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ فَصُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ تِسْعَةِ أَيَّامٍ يَوْمًا وَلَكَ أَجْرُ الثَّمَانِيَةِ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ إِنِّي أَقْوَى مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ صُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ ثَمَانِيَةِ أَيَّامٍ يَوْمًا وَلَكَ أَجْرُ تِلْكَ السَّبْعَةِ قَالَ قُلْتُ إِنِّي أَقْوَى مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ حَتَّى قَالَ صُمْ يَوْمًا وَأَفْطِرْ يَوْمًا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Arim, from Mu'tamir, from his father, from Abu al-'Ala', from Mutarrif, from Ibn Abi Rabi'a — that he mentioned fasting to the Prophet (ﷺ). He said: 'Fast one day out of every ten — and you will have the reward of the [other] nine.' He said: 'I am stronger than that.' He said: 'Fast one day out of every nine — and you will have the reward of eight.' He said: 'I am stronger than that.' He said: 'Fast one day out of every eight — and you will have the reward of [the other] seven.' He said: 'I am stronger than that.' He kept saying this until [the Prophet] said: 'Fast one day and break fast one day.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏بَنِي هَاشِمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَقْلُ ‏ ‏شِبْهِ الْعَمْدِ ‏ ‏مُغَلَّظَةٌ ‏ ‏مِثْلُ ‏ ‏عَقْلِ ‏ ‏الْعَمْدِ وَلَا يُقْتَلُ صَاحِبُهُ وَمَنْ حَمَلَ عَلَيْنَا السِّلَاحَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَّا وَلَا ‏ ‏رَصَدَ ‏ ‏بِطَرِيقٍ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Abu Sa'id, the freed slave of Banu Hashim, from Muhammad ibn Rashid, from Sulayman ibn Musa, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'The blood money for quasi-intentional killing [shibh al-'amd] is augmented — like the blood money for intentional killing — but the perpetrator is not executed. And whoever bears arms against us is not of us, and [whoever lies in ambush] on the road.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَزْهَرُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْمُثَنَّى يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بَابَاهُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَانَ يَقُولُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ ‏يُبَاهِي مَلَائِكَتَهُ عَشِيَّةَ ‏ ‏عَرَفَةَ ‏ ‏بِأَهْلِ ‏ ‏عَرَفَةَ ‏ ‏فَيَقُولُ ‏ ‏انْظُرُوا إِلَى عِبَادِي أَتَوْنِي ‏ ‏شُعْثًا ‏ ‏غُبْرًا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Azhar ibn al-Qasim, from al-Muthanna ibn Sa'id, from Qatada, from 'Abdullah ibn Babah — that the Prophet (ﷺ) would say: 'Allah (عز وجل) boasts of the people of 'Arafa before His angels on the evening of 'Arafa, saying: "Look at My servants — they have come to Me disheveled and dusty."'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ قُتِلَ خَطَأً ‏ ‏فَدِيَتُهُ ‏ ‏مِائَةٌ مِنْ الْإِبِلِ ثَلَاثُونَ ‏ ‏ابْنَةَ مَخَاضٍ ‏ ‏وَثَلَاثُونَ ‏ ‏ابْنَةَ لَبُونٍ ‏ ‏وَثَلَاثُونَ ‏ ‏جَذَعَةً ‏ ‏وَعَشَرَةُ ‏ ‏بَنِي لَبُونٍ ‏ ‏ذُكْرَانٍ فَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُقَوِّمُهَا عَلَى أَثْمَانِ الْإِبِلِ فَإِذَا هَانَتْ نَقَصَ مِنْ قِيمَتِهَا وَإِذَا غَلَتْ رَفَعَ فِي قِيمَتِهَا عَلَى نَحْوِ الزَّمَانِ مَا كَانَتْ فَبَلَغَتْ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا بَيْنَ أَرْبَعِ مِائَةِ دِينَارٍ إِلَى ثَمَانِ مِائَةِ دِينَارٍ أَوْ عِدْلَهَا مِنْ ‏ ‏الْوَرِقِ ‏ ‏ثَمَانِيَةَ آلَافٍ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Abu Sa'id [freed slave of Banu Hashim], from Muhammad ibn Rashid, from Sulayman ibn Musa, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever is killed accidentally — his diya is one hundred camels: thirty bint makhad [she-camels in their second year], thirty bint labun [she-camels in their third year], thirty jidha'a [she-camels in their fifth year], and ten bani labun males [male camels in their third year]. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) would value this according to camel prices — lowering when they fell and raising when they rose, according to the times. In his era it reached between four hundred and eight hundred dinars, or the equivalent in silver: eight thousand [dirhams].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَضَى أَنَّ ‏ ‏الْعَقْلَ ‏ ‏مِيرَاثٌ بَيْنَ وَرَثَةِ الْقَتِيلِ عَلَى فَرَائِضِهِمْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Abu Sa'id, from Muhammad ibn Rashid, from Sulayman ibn Musa, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) ruled: 'The blood money ['aql] is an inheritance [to be distributed] among the heirs of the slain according to their [designated] shares.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَضَى فِي الْأَنْفِ إِذَا ‏ ‏جُدِعَ ‏ ‏كُلُّهُ ‏ ‏الدِّيَةَ ‏ ‏كَامِلَةً وَإِذَا ‏ ‏جُدِعَتْ ‏ ‏أَرْنَبَتُهُ ‏ ‏نِصْفَ ‏ ‏الدِّيَةِ ‏ ‏وَفِي الْعَيْنِ نِصْفَ ‏ ‏الدِّيَةِ ‏ ‏وَفِي الْيَدِ نِصْفَ ‏ ‏الدِّيَةِ ‏ ‏وَفِي الرِّجْلِ نِصْفَ ‏ ‏الدِّيَةِ ‏ ‏وَقَضَى أَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْقِلَ ‏ ‏عَنْ الْمَرْأَةِ ‏ ‏عَصَبَتُهَا ‏ ‏مَنْ كَانُوا وَلَا ‏ ‏يَرِثُوا ‏ ‏مِنْهَا إِلَّا مَا فَضَلَ عَنْ وَرَثَتِهَا وَإِنْ قُتِلَتْ ‏ ‏فَعَقْلُهَا ‏ ‏بَيْنَ وَرَثَتِهَا وَهُمْ يَقْتُلُونَ قَاتِلَهَا وَقَضَى أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَقْلَ ‏ ‏أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ ‏ ‏نِصْفُ ‏ ‏عَقْلِ ‏ ‏الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَهُمْ ‏ ‏الْيَهُودُ ‏ ‏وَالنَّصَارَى ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Abu Sa'id, from Muhammad ibn Rashid, from Sulayman ibn Musa, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) ruled: 'For the nose — if completely amputated: the full blood money. If its tip [al-arnaba] is amputated: half the blood money. For the eye: half the blood money. For the hand: half the blood money. For the leg: half the blood money. He also ruled that a woman's male relatives ['asaba] bear the blood money on her behalf, regardless of who they are — but they do not inherit from her except what remains after her own heirs receive their shares. And if she is killed, her blood money goes to her heirs, and they are the ones who pursue her killer [in retaliation]. He ruled that the blood money of the People of the Book [ahl al-kitab] is half that of the Muslims — meaning the Jews and the Christians.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏بَنِي هَاشِمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شَدَّادٌ أَبُو طَلْحَةَ الرَّاسِبِيُّ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا الْوَازِعِ جَابِرَ بْنَ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا مِنْ قَوْمٍ جَلَسُوا مَجْلِسًا لَمْ يَذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ فِيهِ إِلَّا رَأَوْهُ حَسْرَةً يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Abu Sa'id, the freed slave of Banu Hashim, from Shaddad Abu Talha al-Rasi, who heard Abu al-Wazi' Jabir ibn 'Amr narrating — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: 'No people sit in a gathering in which they do not remember Allah — except that they will see it as a cause of regret on the Day of Resurrection.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏سُئِلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ الرَّجُلِ يَدْخُلُ ‏ ‏الْحَائِطَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَأْكُلُ غَيْرَ مُتَّخِذٍ ‏ ‏خُبْنَةً ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Hammad ibn Khalid, from Hisham ibn Sa'd, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr — that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was asked about a man who enters an orchard [ha'it]. He said: 'He may eat from it without taking anything [out] in his garment [i.e., he may eat on the spot but not take produce away].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي الْوَضَّاحِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ رَافِعٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَنَانُ بْنُ خَارِجَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ ‏ ‏عَلَوِيٌّ ‏ ‏جَرِيءٌ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَخْبِرْنَا عَنْ الْهِجْرَةِ إِلَيْكَ أَيْنَمَا كُنْتَ أَوْ لِقَوْمٍ خَاصَّةً أَمْ إِلَى أَرْضٍ مَعْلُومَةٍ أَمْ إِذَا مُتَّ انْقَطَعَتْ قَالَ فَسَكَتَ عَنْهُ يَسِيرًا ثُمَّ قَالَ أَيْنَ السَّائِلُ قَالَ هَا هُوَ ذَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏الْهِجْرَةُ أَنْ تَهْجُرَ ‏ ‏الْفَوَاحِشَ ‏ ‏مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ وَتُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةَ ‏ ‏وَتُؤْتِيَ الزَّكَاةَ ثُمَّ أَنْتَ مُهَاجِرٌ وَإِنْ مُتَّ بِالْحَضَرِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi, from Muhammad ibn Abi al-Waddah, from al-'Ala' ibn 'Abdullah ibn Rafi', from Hanan ibn Kharija — that a bold, highland Bedouin came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, tell us about hijra [emigration] to you — is it [to be] wherever you are, or for specific people, or to a specific land, or will it end when you die?' He [the Prophet] remained silent briefly, then said: 'Where is the questioner?' He said: 'Here I am, O Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'Hijra is that you abandon indecencies — both what is manifest of them and what is hidden — and that you establish prayer and give zakah. You are then a muhajir [true emigrant], even if you die in a settled town.'

ar: ‏ ‏ثُمَّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏ابْتِدَاءً مِنْ نَفْسِهِ ‏ ‏جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَخْبِرْنَا عَنْ ثِيَابِ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ خَلْقًا تُخْلَقُ أَمْ نَسْجًا تُنْسَجُ فَضَحِكَ بَعْضُ الْقَوْمِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مِمَّ تَضْحَكُونَ مِنْ جَاهِلٍ يَسْأَلُ عَالِمًا ثُمَّ ‏ ‏أَكَبَّ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ قَالَ أَيْنَ السَّائِلُ قَالَ هُوَ ذَا أَنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا بَلْ ‏ ‏تَشَقَّقُ عَنْهَا ثَمَرُ الْجَنَّةِ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ ‏
en: Then 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr volunteered on his own: 'A man came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, tell us about the garments of the people of Paradise — are they created directly or woven?" Some people laughed. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: "What are you laughing at? [Just because] an ignorant person asks a knowledgeable one?" Then the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) leaned forward [in thought], then said: "Where is the questioner?" He said: "Here I am, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "No — rather, they will burst forth from the fruits of Paradise" — [saying it] three times.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ الرَّقِّيُّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَجَّاجُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ ‏ ‏مُثِّلَ ‏ ‏بِهِ أَوْ حُرِّقَ بِالنَّارِ فَهُوَ حُرٌّ وَهُوَ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ قَالَ فَأُتِيَ بِرَجُلٍ قَدْ خُصِيَ يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏سَنْدَرٌ ‏ ‏فَأَعْتَقَهُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ أَتَى ‏ ‏أَبَا بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏بَعْدَ وَفَاةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَصَنَعَ إِلَيْهِ خَيْرًا ‏ ‏ثُمَّ أَتَى ‏ ‏عُمَرَ ‏ ‏بَعْدَ ‏ ‏أَبِي بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏فَصَنَعَ إِلَيْهِ خَيْرًا ثُمَّ إِنَّهُ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ إِلَى ‏ ‏مِصْرَ ‏ ‏فَكَتَبَ لَهُ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ ‏ ‏أَنْ اصْنَعْ بِهِ خَيْرًا أَوْ احْفَظْ وَصِيَّةَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِيهِ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Ma'mar ibn Sulayman al-Raqqi, from al-Hajjaj, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ): 'Whoever is mutilated [by his master] — or burned with fire — he is free and is the mawla [client/freedman] of Allah and His Messenger.' Then a man who had been castrated — called Sandar — was brought [to the Prophet (ﷺ)], and he freed him. Then [Sandar] came to Abu Bakr after the death of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), and [Abu Bakr] treated him with kindness. Then he came to 'Umar after Abu Bakr, and ['Umar] treated him with kindness. Then he wished to travel to Egypt, and 'Umar wrote to 'Amr ibn al-'As: 'Treat him with kindness and honor the bequest of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) concerning him.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَجَّاجُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏الرَّجُلُ يَغِيبُ لَا يَقْدِرُ عَلَى الْمَاءِ أَيُجَامِعُ أَهْلَهُ قَالَ نَعَمْ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As (رضي الله عنهما) — via Ma'mar ibn Sulayman, from al-Hajjaj, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather — that a man came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, a man goes away [on a journey] and cannot access water — may he have intercourse with his wife?' He said: 'Yes.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زِيَادِ بْنِ فَيَّاضٍ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا عِيَاضٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ لَهُ صُمْ يَوْمًا وَلَكَ أَجْرُ مَا بَقِيَ قَالَ إِنِّي أُطِيقُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ صُمْ يَوْمَيْنِ وَلَكَ أَجْرُ مَا بَقِيَ قَالَ إِنِّي أُطِيقُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ صُمْ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ وَلَكَ أَجْرُ مَا بَقِيَ قَالَ إِنِّي أُطِيقُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ صُمْ أَرْبَعَةَ أَيَّامٍ وَلَكَ أَجْرُ مَا بَقِيَ قَالَ إِنِّي أُطِيقُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ ‏ ‏صُمْ أَفْضَلَ الصِّيَامِ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ صُمْ صَوْمَ ‏ ‏دَاوُدَ ‏ ‏كَانَ يَصُومُ يَوْمًا وَيُفْطِرُ يَوْمًا ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Muhammad ibn Ja'far, from Shu'ba, from Ziyad ibn Fayyad, who heard Abu 'Iyad narrating from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr — that the Prophet (ﷺ) said to him: 'Fast one day and you will have the reward of what remains [of the year].' He said: 'I am capable of more than that.' He said: 'Fast two days and you will have the reward of what remains.' He said: 'I am capable of more.' He said: 'Fast three days and you will have the reward of what remains.' He said: 'I am capable of more.' He said: 'Fast four days and you will have the reward of what remains.' He said: 'I am capable of more.' He said: 'Fast the best of fasting with Allah — fast the fast of Dawud. He would fast one day and break fast one day.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَارِمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُعْتَمِرٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْحَضْرَمِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنْ الْمُسْلِمِينَ اسْتَأْذَنَ نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي امْرَأَةٍ يُقَالُ لَهَا ‏ ‏أُمُّ مَهْزُولٍ ‏ ‏كَانَتْ ‏ ‏تُسَافِحُ ‏ ‏وَتَشْتَرِطُ لَهُ أَنْ تُنْفِقَ عَلَيْهِ وَأَنَّهُ اسْتَأْذَنَ فِيهَا النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَوْ ذَكَرَ لَهُ أَمْرَهَا ‏ ‏فَقَرَأَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ { ‏الزَّانِيَةُ لَا يَنْكِحُهَا إِلَّا زَانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ ‏} ‏قَالَ أُنْزِلَتْ ‏ { ‏الزَّانِيَةُ لَا يَنْكِحُهَا إِلَّا زَانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ ‏} ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَارِمٌ ‏ ‏سَأَلْتُ ‏ ‏مُعْتَمِرًا ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏الْحَضْرَمِيِّ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ قَاصًّا وَقَدْ رَأَيْتُهُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ مَعِينٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْمُعْتَمِرُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَضْرَمِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏نَحْوَهُ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via 'Arim, from Mu'tamir, who said: my father narrated from al-Hadrami, from al-Qasim ibn Muhammad, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr — that a man from among the Muslims sought the Prophet's (ﷺ) permission [to marry] a woman called Umm Mahzul, who was a fornicatress and had stipulated that she would spend on him. He sought the Prophet's permission or mentioned her matter to him. The Prophet (ﷺ) recited: {The adulteress shall not be married except by an adulterer or an idolater} [24:3]. He said: It was revealed — {The adulteress shall not be married except by an adulterer or an idolater}. ['Arim said: I asked Mu'tamir about al-Hadrami and he said: He was a storyteller [qass] and I have seen him. 'Abdullah (son of Ahmad) adds: Also narrated via Yahya ibn Ma'in, from al-Mu'tamir, from his father, from al-Hadrami, from al-Qasim ibn Muhammad, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr — similarly.]

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَهْبُ بْنُ جَرِيرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏الصَّقْعَبَ بْنَ زُهَيْرٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَعْرَابِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏جُبَّةٌ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏طَيَالِسَةٍ ‏ ‏مَكْفُوفَةٌ ‏ ‏بِدِيبَاجٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ مَزْرُورَةٌ ‏ ‏بِدِيبَاجٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنَّ صَاحِبَكُمْ هَذَا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَرْفَعَ كُلَّ رَاعٍ ابْنِ رَاعٍ وَيَضَعَ كُلَّ فَارِسٍ ابْنِ فَارِسٍ فَقَامَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مُغْضَبًا فَأَخَذَ بِمَجَامِعِ ‏ ‏جُبَّتِهِ ‏ ‏فَاجْتَذَبَهُ وَقَالَ لَا أَرَى عَلَيْكَ ثِيَابَ مَنْ لَا يَعْقِلُ ثُمَّ رَجَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَجَلَسَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ ‏ ‏نُوحًا ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ السَّلَام ‏ ‏لَمَّا حَضَرَتْهُ الْوَفَاةُ دَعَا ابْنَيْهِ فَقَالَ إِنِّي قَاصِرٌ عَلَيْكُمَا الْوَصِيَّةَ آمُرُكُمَا بِاثْنَتَيْنِ وَأَنْهَاكُمَا عَنْ اثْنَتَيْنِ أَنْهَاكُمَا عَنْ الشِّرْكِ وَالْكِبْرِ وَآمُرُكُمَا ‏ ‏بِلَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فَإِنَّ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَمَا فِيهِمَا لَوْ وُضِعَتْ فِي كِفَّةِ الْمِيزَانِ وَوُضِعَتْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فِي الْكِفَّةِ الْأُخْرَى كَانَتْ أَرْجَحَ وَلَوْ أَنَّ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ كَانَتَا حَلْقَةً فَوُضِعَتْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ عَلَيْهَا لَفَصَمَتْهَا ‏ ‏أَوْ لَقَصَمَتْهَا ‏ ‏وَآمُرُكُمَا بِسُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ فَإِنَّهَا صَلَاةُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَبِهَا يُرْزَقُ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ ‏
en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr (رضي الله عنهما) — via Wahb ibn Jarir, from his father, who heard al-Saq'ab ibn Zuhayr narrating from Zayd ibn Aslam, from 'Ata' ibn Yasar, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr — that a Bedouin came to the Prophet (ﷺ) wearing a jubba of taylasana fabric, lined or buttoned with dibaj [silk brocade]. He said: 'Your companion here wants to elevate every shepherd son of a shepherd, and bring down every horseman son of a horseman [i.e., reverse the social order].' The Prophet (ﷺ) rose up angered, seized the front of his jubba and pulled him, and said: 'I do not see on you the garments of one who has no understanding.' Then the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) returned and sat down, and said: 'Indeed, Nuh (عليه السلام), when death came to him, called his two sons and said: "I am giving you a brief testament — I command you with two things and I forbid you from two things. I forbid you from shirk and arrogance. And I command you with La ilaha illa Allah — for if the heavens and the earth and all that is in them were placed in one pan of the scale, and La ilaha illa Allah were placed in the other pan, it would outweigh them. And if the heavens and the earth were [like] a ring and La ilaha illa Allah were placed upon it, it would crack it apart [or: shatter it].
